summary: API shape, validation, server-only Prisma boundary, structured errors, and scope are mostly sound, but DELETE currently hard-deletes through cascade and can destroy historical run data; handoff branch metadata is also inaccurate.

tests_run.result: Next.js production build passed with non-fatal SWC warnings.

decisions: Treat hard-delete cascade as a required architecture fix because historical run data preservation is documented.
Keep the task within the current no-auth/no-UI/no-scan/no-Prisma-schema-change boundary.
risks: Raw unexpected errors are logged server-side, though sanitized from API responses.
No API integration tests were added; only required validation/build commands were run.
requested_action: Change DELETE to preserve historical data or reject destructive deletes with a structured error, then correct branch/worktree metadata and rerun required validation.
